title: carlina brevibracteata
intro: Carlina brevibracteata, also known as the short-bracted carline thistle, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Carlina brevibracteata include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat respiratory problems and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
